title: rubus silvaticus subsp. macrophyllus
intro: Rubus silvaticus subsp. macrophyllus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan wild raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus silvaticus plant, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits. The leaves, roots, and fruits of Rubus silvaticus subsp. macrophyllus are used to prepare herbal remedies, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. Traditional herbal preparations involve using the plant's leaves and roots to make teas, salves, and ointments that may help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
